skill.name: anndata
skill.description: Guidance for creating, reading, writing, subsetting, and combining AnnData objects for single-cell and scverse workflows.
skill.summary: Work with AnnData, h5ad files, and annotated matrices for single-cell analysis.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution and high-severity keyword matches, but review found Markdown code fences and AnnData examples rather than executable shell logic. The real risk is low and limited to legitimate local file I/O and optional remote dataset loading examples. No prompt injection, credential access, exfiltration, obfuscation, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: FALSE POSITIVE: Markdown code fences classified as shell execution
low_findings.description: The external_commands hits point to Markdown code fences and Python AnnData examples, not Ruby backtick execution. No shell command construction or user-controlled command execution was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Representative locations show Markdown fences and Python examples in documentation files. There is no executable wrapper, shell interpolation, or command runner in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: LOW: Remote dataset access examples require trusted sources
low_findings.description: The skill demonstrates fsspec and urllib access to example dataset URLs. This is legitimate AnnData I/O guidance, but users should only load trusted remote files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code shows real network-capable APIs, but the endpoint is example.com and no secrets are collected or transmitted. The behavior is consistent with documented AnnData remote I/O.

low_findings.title: LOW: Local file read and write examples need normal user consent
low_findings.description: The skill includes examples for reading, writing, appending, and converting local h5ad, zarr, CSV, HDF5, and related files. These are core AnnData functions, not hidden filesystem access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines directly show local AnnData file operations. They are transparent examples for user-requested data work, with no evidence of stealthy access.

low_findings.title: FALSE POSITIVE: Crypto, C2, and reconnaissance keyword matches are benign
low_findings.description: High-severity keyword hits come from scientific metadata terms such as key, description, hash-like identifiers, and memory guidance. No weak cryptographic use, C2 behavior, or reconnaissance logic was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sampled static locations contain AnnData metadata and memory-efficiency documentation. There are no cryptographic APIs, beaconing endpoints, or host-enumeration commands in context.

content.value_statement: Single-cell datasets are hard to manage when metadata, matrices, and embeddings live in separate formats. This skill provides AnnData patterns for h5ad I/O, annotated matrices, subsetting, concatenation, and memory-aware workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains AnnData components such as X, obs, var, layers, obsm, obsp, uns, and raw.
Shows how to read and write h5ad, zarr, CSV, Loom, MTX, 10X, and HDF5 data.
Provides examples for backed mode, sparse matrices, categoricals, and chunked processing.
Guides concatenation across samples, batches, variables, layers, and on-disk datasets.
Covers subsetting, copying, metadata updates, renaming, filtering, and type conversion.
Connects AnnData usage with Scanpy, Muon, and PyTorch data loading patterns.
limitations: It is reference guidance and does not execute analysis by itself.
It does not replace Scanpy documentation for full preprocessing or clustering workflows.
It does not validate biological assumptions or experimental design choices.
Large file workflows still depend on local memory, storage, and installed package versions.

use_cases.title: Prepare h5ad Files
use_cases.description: Create AnnData objects with aligned expression matrices, cell metadata, gene metadata, layers, and embeddings.

use_cases.title: Combine Experiment Batches
use_cases.description: Concatenate multiple AnnData objects while tracking batch labels, join behavior, metadata merges, and index conflicts.

use_cases.title: Handle Large Datasets
use_cases.description: Use backed mode, sparse matrices, categorical metadata, chunking, and on-disk concatenation for memory-aware processing.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic AnnData Object
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to create an AnnData object from a matrix with cell and gene metadata.

prompt_templates.title: Read and Write h5ad Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me read a large h5ad file, inspect metadata, and save a compressed output file.

prompt_templates.title: Concatenate Multiple Samples
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan an AnnData concatenation workflow for three batches with batch labels and unique cell names.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Scalable AnnData Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my planned AnnData pipeline for memory efficiency, backed mode, sparse storage, metadata consistency, and reproducibility.

output_examples.input: I have counts, cell metadata, and gene metadata. How should I store them?
output_examples.output: Use X for the count matrix, obs for cell metadata, var for gene metadata, and layers for alternate matrices.

output_examples.input: My h5ad file is too large for memory.
output_examples.output: Open it in backed read mode, inspect metadata first, subset by observations or variables, then load only the needed subset.

output_examples.input: I need to merge three single-cell batches.
output_examples.output: Concatenate along observations, add a batch label, choose inner or outer joins deliberately, and keep observation names unique.

best_practices: Use backed mode, sparse matrices, and categoricals before loading large single-cell datasets into memory.
Preserve raw counts before filtering or normalization when later comparisons may need original values.
Track batch, sample, and modality labels during concatenation so downstream analysis remains interpretable.
anti_patterns: Loading very large h5ad files fully into memory before checking metadata or required subsets.
Concatenating datasets without unique observation names or explicit batch labels.
Writing dense matrices when the expression data is sparse and suitable for compressed storage.

faq.question: What is AnnData used for?
faq.answer: AnnData stores annotated matrices with observations, variables, metadata, embeddings, layers, and unstructured analysis results.

faq.question: Does this skill run Scanpy analysis?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on AnnData storage and manipulation patterns. Scanpy covers full analysis workflows.

faq.question: Can it help with large h5ad files?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains backed mode, chunked access, sparse matrices, categoricals, and on-disk concatenation.

faq.question: Can it combine multiple AnnData objects?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers observation and variable concatenation, join strategies, metadata merges, batch labels, and index handling.

faq.question: Does it support cloud data?
faq.answer: It includes examples for remote Zarr and URL-based file access. Users should only load trusted sources.

faq.question: What should I use for probabilistic single-cell models?
faq.answer: Use scvi-tools for probabilistic models. This skill helps prepare and manage the AnnData objects those tools consume.
